projects.name: Solar + Energy Storage on Garage
projects.description: Install 5-7 solar panels on south-facing garage roof face (20x12 ft, ~2-2.8 kW) plus battery storage. Solar = standalone ROI investment (~5yr payback at $0.42/kWh). Battery = generator alternative (no lot space for generator). 30% federal ITC applies to both when paired.
projects.notes: DESIGN: South garage face only. 5 portrait panels = 2.0 kW; 7 mixed = 2.8 kW. ~3,200-4,500 kWh/yr production (20-26% offset). All-in after ITC: solar ~$9-10k, battery ~$9-10k. VENDORS: Sunergy (sunergy.com) — EnergySage 2026 CA Installer of Year, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door. NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com) — Diamond Certified, 35yr, San Mateo County, 40yr warranty. Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy) — online only. AVOID: Sunrun.

tasks.title: Analyze energy usage and size system
tasks.notes: 17,178 kWh/yr at $0.42/kWh avg. Target: 5-7 panels south garage face (20x12 ft) = 2-2.8 kW. ~5yr payback after ITC.

tasks.title: Research and shortlist solar installers
tasks.notes: Shortlist: Sunergy (sunergy.com, Enphase Platinum, no door-to-door), NRG Clean Power (nrgcleanpower.com, Diamond Certified, San Mateo County), Tesla Energy (tesla.com/energy, online only). Avoid Sunrun.

tasks.title: Contact installers for quotes
tasks.notes: Spec: south garage face (20x12 ft, 5-7 panels, ~2-2.8 kW), paired battery (Enphase IQ or Powerwall 3), detached garage sub-panel interconnect. Ask each for: $/W, panel brand, inverter type, battery spec, warranty, timeline, ITC total. Request free shade/LIDAR analysis.

tasks.title: Compare quotes and select installer
tasks.notes: Prefer Enphase microinverters over string inverter. Compare $/W, battery spec, warranty, timeline. 30% ITC on full system incl. battery.

tasks.title: Confirm ITC timing — credit files in year PTO is granted
tasks.notes: 30% federal ITC on Form 5695. Claimed in tax year Permission to Operate granted by PG&E. Battery qualifies only when solar-paired. Plan install timing accordingly.

insights.summary: The sustained high presence count (13-15 devices) combined with zero camera activity confirms a technical outage (likely Frigate or network) rather than a household event, as occupancy data verifies normal indoor activity and effectively rules out a security breach.

The consistently high presence count (11-14 devices) confirms the entire household is indoors, but combined with the inactive cameras and flagged restless dog pattern, it creates a complete outdoor monitoring blind spot. You are missing visual context for what is causing the dogs' unusual behavior right now.
No camera observations recorded today despite cameras typically being active. Check Frigate is running: `docker ps | grep frigate`

The temporary dip to 12 connected devices at 09:42 likely corresponds to household members stepping outside, which aligns with the flagged pattern of the dogs being unusually active in the backyard. This confirms a normal Saturday morning routine rather than a network or security issue.
Pattern: dogs have been unusually active in the backyard on 6 of the last 7 days (avg 6 observations/day). This may indicate fence activity, a neighborhood animal, or family routine changes.

The stable presence of 14–16 devices combined with zero camera activity confirms the entire household is still indoors and undisturbed, indicating Roxanne’s sleep loss is likely isolated to her personal tracker rather than an environmental or security issue.
Roxanne slept only 1.76h last night. May be a rough morning — heads up.

The sustained presence of 13–15 mobile devices alongside zero camera motion and no dog activity suggests a presence detection inaccuracy or offline cameras, which could cause occupancy-based automations to run incorrectly. Verifying camera status and presence calibration will prevent unnecessary energy use or false security alerts.

The sustained presence of 14–17 devices alongside zero camera motion and no dog activity strongly suggests the household is gathered in non-monitored interior spaces (e.g., bedrooms, basement, or home office) for typical Friday evening downtime, rather than indicating a system anomaly or absence.

The stable presence of 14-17 devices alongside zero camera activity and no dog sightings indicates the family is likely congregating in a camera-blind zone (e.g., basement, garage, or backyard) or that camera feeds are silently failing. This matters because it creates a persistent coverage gap that could mask normal household activity or early warning signs.
